text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful framework is the SMART approach: goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase website traffic by 20% within six months.” To stay on track, break the project into milestones—these are key checkpoints, like completing the design phase. Each milestone should produce specific deliverables, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, so that expectations match and resources are properly allocated.
